column11: Narjes Aljunibi, known as Nar, is a DJ, visual artist, and sensory explorer based in Fort Lee. With a deep passion for sound, movement, and immersive experiences, they blend ambient textures, deep grooves, and genre-fluid rhythms to evoke nostalgia and belonging. Their DJ show, Homemade, transforms the essence of home into sound, incorporating visual art and multi-sensory elements to create a fully immersive experience. Inspired by the intersections of culture, memory, and space, Nar invites listeners on a journey through sound that feels both personal and universal.

YANAIV brings high-quality driving, groovy beats with distinctive melodies and sharp high frequencies. Ukrainian born, US based techno dj whose passion for the craft was born in The Netherlands 3 years ago. 
 I strongly believe in the freeing power of the underground and dancefloor’s superiority and always strives to serve the crowd with the flow of earcandy tunes that nurture the mind and body of every present soul.
 Expect high-quality driving, groovy beats with distinctive melodies and sharp high frequencies.

column11: Originally from Las Vegas. I make music and art inspired by Kalahari Oyster Cult, Animal Collective, Lightning Bolt, etc.

column10: Monthly on the First & Third Wednesday @ Noon
column11: Hi,  Hope all is well. My name is Ephraim. I come from a large family of musicians. I got into DJ from family and friends. I currently co-own a brand in Atlanta called Player99. We are a DJ collective there that helps program parties and events for the next generation of Atlanta creatives and young professionals. I currently live in Portland and I’m looking to DJ more outside of Jackie’s and Two Wrongs. Would love to work with you all to program a radio that I started on Player 99 Radio called K&N.

I first got into music through a small country church in Mississippi, but after Katrina, my family relocated to Seattle. I spent a few years as a jazz vocalist before transitioning into teaching—I’m now a music industry teacher at Tacoma School of the Arts. DJing wasn’t originally on my radar, but my partner would always come home excited about a new track or mix, and I wanted to understand that passion and support him better. That curiosity led me here, and now I run a party called "Ghetto Tech". As a DJ, I blend house, club, and Ghetto Tech, creating sets that move between soulful grooves, high-energy rhythms, and the raw, fast-paced sounds of the underground.

I have been in the Seattle music community since I was 14 attending raves and participating in online forums. I cannot stick to one genre to save my life so through the years I have participated in many scenes in area, from dnb, to club, to bass, even happy hardcore. I believe my strong suit is blending together genres and scenes that art first don't seem compatible. My main inspo for this show would be the NTS Breakfast and Soup to Nuts shows (I LOVE radio)
